title: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking, rural (% of rural population)
definition: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking, rural is the proportion of rural population primarily using clean cooking fuels and technologies for cooking. Under WHO guidelines, kerosene is excluded from clean cooking fuels.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for rural clean cooking access.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include United States, Uruguay, Trinidad and Tobago, while the lightest include Central African Republic, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the rural clean cooking access pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Rural clean cooking access may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Clean cooking fuels access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Urban clean cooking access can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
GNI per capita, PPP can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Energy maps often separate access, consumption, and production mix.
Look for hydro, fossil-fuel, and low-access signals.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: near-duplicate of clean fuels access with an even narrower rural slice.
Retired for beta so the catalog does not feel padded with obvious variants.
